query: Gemini 3.5 Flash Omni Google I/O 2026 specifications benchmarks

{"query":"Gemini 3.5 Flash Omni Google I/O 2026 specifications benchmarks","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1600,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/gemini-models/gemini-3-5/","title":"Gemini 3.5: frontier intelligence with action","content":"May 19, 2026 — It's our strongest agentic and coding model yet, agentic benchmarks like Terminal-Bench 2.1 (76.2%), GDPval-AA (1656 Elo) and MCP Atlas (83.6%) ...
It’s our strongest agentic and coding model yet, outperforming Gemini 3.1 Pro on challenging coding and agentic benchmarks like Terminal-Bench 2.1 (76.2%), GDPval-AA (1656 Elo) and MCP Atlas (83.6%), and leading in multimodal understanding (84.2% on CharXiv Reasoning).
When looking at output tokens per second, it is 4 times faster than other frontier models.
Under supervision, it can reliably execute multi-step workflows and coding tasks while sustaining frontier performance.
At I/O today, we showed how its agentic capabilities are powering new features to bring frontier-level intelligence to your daily life.
The enhanced agentic coding capabilities of 3.5 Flash are also delivering even more intelligent experiences across Search, from introducing new information agents that work for you 24/7 to unlocking more dynamic generative UI experiences."},{"url":"https://kenhuangus.substack.com/p/google-io-2026-was-not-just-a-model","title":"Google I/O 2026 Was Not Just a Model Launch. It Was Google Showing the Agent Stack.","content":"Google I/O 2026 had plenty of demos.
It is the stack formed by Gemini 3.5 Flash, Antigravity, Gemini Spark, AI Mode in Search, Gemini Omni, Chrome, and developer-facing managed agents.
The old boundary was the app: Search, Gmail, Docs, YouTube, Android Studio.
Gemini 3.5 Flash benchmark readout.
The cost caveat is important: Artificial Analysis says the model costs $1,552 to run its Intelligence Index, which is 5.5 times Gemini 3 Flash and 75% more than Gemini 3.1 Pro in that test suite.
Generative UI is planned for everyone in Search this summer at no charge, while persistent custom experiences with Antigravity begin first for Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers in the U.S."},{"url":"https://mashable.com/article/google-io-2026-gemini-35-flash","title":"Google launches Gemini 3.5 Flash. How to try it for free. | Mashable","content":"In the keynote address of Google I/O 2026, Google CEO Sundar Pichai announced that Gemini 3.5 flash is launching immediately via the Gemini app and other Google products.
"We are also excited for 3.5 Pro,” said Sundar Pichai, CEO of Google and Alphabet, at a pre-Google I/O media briefing.
The Gemini AI chatbot now has 900 million monthly active users, up from 400 million at this time last year, according to Google.
Follow the Mashable Google I/O live blog to see all of the latest news on Gemini, Chrome, and Android.
The first model, Gemini Omni Flash, rolls out today.
Google didn't launch Gemini 3.5 Pro at Google I/O."},{"url":"https://www.chatprd.ai/how-i-ai/google-io-new-ai-tools-gemini-35-flash-to-omni-video","title":"How I AI: My Live Test of Google I/O’s New AI Tools—From Gemini 3.5 Flash to Omni Video | ChatPRD Blog","content":"It was a lot to take in, with so many product names that it’s hard to keep them all straight—Gemini, Antigravity, Flow, Stitch, Pomelli, and Omni, just to name a few.
The benchmarks position it as rivaling top models in intelligence while being significantly faster.
The best place to test this is in their agentic IDE, Antigravity.
Pomelli is for generating brand assets, and Stitch is for designing UIs.
Pomelli: I gave it my website, ChatPRD."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Gemini 3.5 Flash Omni Google I/O 2026 specifications benchmarks","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://llm-stats.com/blog/research/gemini-3.5-flash-launch","title":"Gemini 3.5 Flash: Benchmarks, Pricing, and Complete Specs","content":"# Gemini 3.5 Flash: Benchmarks, Pricing, and Complete Specs. Gemini 3.5 Flash is GA today. $1.50/$9 per 1M tokens, 1M context, 76.2% Terminal-Bench 2.1, beats Gemini 3.1 Pro on coding and agents. Google releasedGemini 3.5 Flash today at Google I/O 2026. It is the first model in the new **Gemini 3.5 family** and the strongest agentic and coding model the Flash series has ever shipped. Generally available immediately via the Gemini API, Google AI Studio, Google Antigravity, the Gemini app, and AI Mode in Google Search. A 3.5 Pro version is in internal use and slated for next month. The headline claim fromGoogle's announcement is unusual for a Flash release: **3.5 Flash beats Gemini 3.1 Pro**on most coding and agentic benchmarks, while running roughly 4x faster than comparable frontier models, and often at less than half the cost. Gemini 3.5 Flash · May 19, 2026. Beats Gemini 3.1 Pro on the coding and agentic suite. 3.1 Pro  →  3.5 Flash.","score":0.8875269,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://mashable.com/article/google-io-2026-gemini-35-flash","title":"Google launches Gemini 3.5 Flash. How to try it for free.","content":"# Google launches Gemini 3.5 Flash model. How to try it for free now. Google CEO Sundar Pichai introduces Gemini 3.5 Flash at Google I/O 2026. Google just unveiled a new family of models for Gemini at its annual Google I/O developers conference: Gemini 3.5. The first model will be Gemini 3.5 Flash, a new AI model that's available now at no cost to billions of people around the world. In the keynote address of Google I/O 2026, Google CEO Sundar Pichai announced that Gemini 3.5 flash is launching immediately via the Gemini app and other Google products. \"We are also excited for 3.5 Pro,” said Sundar Pichai, CEO of Google and Alphabet, at a pre-Google I/O media briefing. Gemini 3.5 Flash is the first model in the 3.5 family, and Google says it outperforms Gemini 3.1 Pro on most benchmarks while also delivering much faster performance.","score":0.7576693,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://kenhuangus.substack.com/p/google-io-2026-was-not-just-a-model","title":"Google I/O 2026 Was Not Just a Model Launch. It Was Google Showing the Agent Stack.","content":"# Google I/O 2026 Was Not Just a Model Launch. The useful read is simpler and more technical: Google is trying to turn Gemini from a chatbot family into a **distributed agent runtime**. It is the stack formed by **Gemini 3.5 Flash**, **Antigravity**, **Gemini Spark**, **AI Mode in Search**, **Gemini Omni**, Chrome, and developer-facing managed agents. The paid section goes deeper into the technical readout: why “Flash” no longer means low-end, why benchmark wins are less important than throughput-per-task, how Antigravity turns agents into an execution substrate, why Search-generated mini-apps could change software distribution, where Gemini Omni is genuinely hard, and what builders should actually do next. Gemini 3.5 Flash is the reasoning and action model. The developer announcement used a similar phrase: Google is moving “from prompts to action” with Gemini 3.5 Flash, Antigravity 2.0, Managed Agents in the Gemini API, and AI Studio updates. Google introduced Gemini 3.5 Flash as the first Gemini 3.5 release and positioned it as a model built for complex agentic workflows.","score":0.69573146,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.chatprd.ai/how-i-ai/google-io-new-ai-tools-gemini-35-flash-to-omni-video","title":"How I AI: My Live Test of Google I/O’s New AI Tools—From Gemini 3.5 Flash to Omni Video | ChatPRD Blog","content":"←All articlesBack/How I AI/How I AI: My Live Test of Google I/O’s New AI Tools—From Gemini 3.5 Flash to Omni Video. # How I AI: My Live Test of Google I/O’s New AI Tools—From Gemini 3.5 Flash to Omni Video. I went hands-on with the biggest announcements from Google I/O 2026, testing the new Gemini 3.5 Flash model in Anti-Gravity, creating videos with Omni, and seeing what actually works (and what doesn't). Today was the first day of Google I/O 2026, and the announcements came fast and furious. We saw new models, new developer tools, and a whole suite of creative features. It was a lot to take in, with so many product names that it’s hard to keep them all straight—Gemini, Antigravity, Flow, Stitch, Pomelli, and Omni, just to name a few. In the spirit of *How I AI*, I couldn't just read the press releases. ### Workflow 1: Building an API with Antigravity 2.0 & Gemini 3.5 Flash.","score":0.65966886,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/gemini-models/gemini-3-5","title":"Gemini 3.5: frontier intelligence with action - Google Blog","content":"[Skip to main content](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/gemini-models/gemini-3-5#jump-content).
